title: Spring.
content:
I am a virgin, whom no man hath known,
    And all desire to know. The figure I
    Of mortal dream and mortal prophecy.
    Thou desert Sphinx, with thy gray lips of stone,
    Keep thy poor secret, I have kept mine own!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Margaret Steele Anderson, titled "Spring.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
